info_text: This adapter automatically controls your Zendure Solarflow battery to achieve zero grid feed-in/draw. It reads grid power and adjusts battery charge/discharge accordingly.
zendureSolarflowInstance_help: The instance name of the nograx zendure-solarflow adapter
deviceProductKey_help: The ProductKey of your Zendure device (e.g., for 1600AC+)
deviceKey_help: The DeviceKey of your Zendure device
deviceHint_text: 💡 You can find ProductKey and DeviceKey in the zendure-solarflow adapter object tree. Look for the device path structure.
powerMeterDp_help: Select the datapoint that provides current grid power (positive = drawing from grid, negative = feeding to grid)
targetGridPowerW_help: Target grid power in watts. 0 = zero grid draw/feed, positive = allow some grid draw, negative = allow some grid feed
updateIntervalSec_help: How often to update battery power (recommended: 5 seconds)
minBatterySoc_help: Stop discharging when SOC falls below this value (when using SOC protection mode)
maxBatterySoc_help: Stop charging when SOC reaches this value (maxVol is ignored, full charge = 100% SOC)
hysteresisW_help: Minimum power change required to update (avoids frequent small adjustments)
rampUpWPerCycle_help: Maximum power increase per cycle (0 = unlimited). Applies to discharge increase AND charge decrease
rampDownWPerCycle_help: Maximum power decrease per cycle (0 = unlimited). Applies to discharge decrease AND charge increase
tuningHint_text: ⚙️ Ramp rates control how quickly the battery power can change. Higher values = faster response but more aggressive. Lower values = smoother but slower response.
emergencyChargeVoltageV_help: When ANY pack drops below this critical voltage, emergency charging starts (typical: 2.8V for LFP)
emergencyChargePowerW_help: Charging power during emergency mode (ignores grid target)
emergencyExitSoc_help: At this SOC level, emergency charging ends and recovery mode begins
emergencyRecoverySoc_help: Until this SOC is reached, only charging is allowed (no discharge). Prevents endless loop after emergency
useLowVoltageBlock_help: When device signals 'lowVoltageBlock=true', stop discharging immediately (device protection)
useFullChargeNeeded_help: When device signals 'fullChargeNeeded=true', force full charge cycle (battery calibration)
dischargeProtectionMode_help: Choose how to protect battery from deep discharge: by SOC percentage or by pack cell voltage
minBatteryVoltageV_help: Stop discharging when ANY pack falls below this voltage (voltage mode only). Typical: 3.0V for LFP
emergencyCharging_hint: 🚨 Emergency charging activates on: critical pack voltage, device flags (lowVoltageBlock/fullChargeNeeded). Has highest priority and ignores grid target!
emergencyRecovery_hint: 🔄 Recovery phase: After emergency (from exit SOC), battery continues charging with normal grid target, but discharge is blocked until recovery SOC. Prevents endless loop.
feedInThresholdW_help: Only start charging when grid feed-in exceeds this threshold. Negative value (e.g. -150W = 150W feed-in)
feedInDelayTicks_help: Number of cycles feed-in must persist before switching to charging. Prevents frequent switching on short grid fluctuations (1 tick = update interval)
switchProtection_hint: ⚡ Protects hardware relays from excessive switching. Battery only switches to charging after sustained grid feed-in exceeds threshold for specified ticks. Reduces mechanical wear!
setPowerMaxRetries_help: How many times to retry if device doesn't accept power setpoint (only for charging)
setPowerValidationToleranceW_help: Acceptable deviation between setpoint and actual power
